commit.message:
feat: FinanceWatch AI — full application with security audit fixes

Full-stack financial intelligence platform (FastAPI + React + PostgreSQL)
with 24 security/performance audit fixes applied:

- Security: HTML injection prevention, rate limiting, token revocation,
  CSP/HSTS headers, file magic bytes validation, removed default passwords
- Performance: SQL aggregation replacing in-memory processing, N+1 query
  fixes via JOINs and correlated subqueries, database indexes
- Architecture: Pydantic enum validation, safe UUID parsing, async email
  via ThreadPoolExecutor, pagination, comment length validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
research: formalize Cauchy-Schwarz equality characterization (cauchy-schwarz-oq-03-oq-01) (#3374)

Proves when equality holds in Cauchy-Schwarz via the Lagrange identity:
- Lagrange identity: ∑ᵢ∑ⱼ(fᵢgⱼ-fⱼgᵢ)² = 2[(∑f²)(∑g²)-(∑fg)²]
- Full iff: CS equality ⟺ all cross-terms vanish (proportionality)
- Inner product space: ‖⟨u,v⟩‖ = ‖u‖·‖v‖ ⟺ ∃c, u = c•v
- Independent CS proof from Lagrange identity
- Hölder inequality for NNReal finite sums

10+ theorems, 0 sorries, Docker-verified.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify API version tracking: inline extraction in APIIndex.load()

Remove extract_version_from_path() helper function and inline the version
extraction directly in APIIndex.load() where we already have the file Path
objects. This is simpler and avoids an unnecessary helper function since
version extraction only happens once at API load time.

The extraction logic (e.g., liblcm_api_v11.0.0.json -> 11.0.0) is now
inline at each location where we load an API file.

commit.message:
fix: raise exec_timeout to 300s and job_timeout to 900s

skill install (git clone + uv sync + deps.sh) easily exceeds 120s on
slow machines. 300s per command and 900s per job gives enough headroom
without hurting fast commands (they finish immediately regardless).

feat(gateway): HEGEMON metering + fleet dashboard (CAB-1720, CAB-1721) (#1543)

## Summary
- Agent lifecycle events via in-memory ring buffer (1000 events) + Kafka
topic `hegemon.agent_events`
- Fleet dashboard endpoint with aggregated status from all HEGEMON
subsystems
- Events: agent_authenticated, dispatch_started/completed,
claim_reserved/released, budget_checked/recorded, supervision_blocked
- GET /admin/hegemon/dashboard — fleet status JSON
- GET /admin/hegemon/events?since=&limit= — event history

Part of HEGEMON Agent Gateway MEGA (CAB-1709), Phase 5.

## Test plan
- [x] 12 metering tests (buffer eviction, capacity, concurrency,
serialization)
- [x] 4 dashboard tests (serialization, empty state, multiple agents)
- [x] Full test suite: 1639 unit + 31 contract + 49 integration + 15
resilience + 22 security
- [x] `cargo clippy` zero warnings
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` clean
- [ ] CI green

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix search blank screen and duplicate display IDs

- MemberAvatar: guard against null/undefined/non-string name to prevent
  TypeError crash when usedBy array contains bad data from Sheets;
  this was the root cause of the blank screen during search
- GAS addItem: use LockService to serialize concurrent item additions,
  then generate displayId server-side from actual sheet state (not
  client-provided value) so two simultaneous adds never get the same
  number; return the assigned displayId to the client
- Frontend: update item's displayId in state when GAS returns a
  different one than the client optimistically assigned

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Instead of fast-exiting for loki/grafana/prometheus/nginx, resolve their

provisioning/) and let the LLM patch them like any other file.

  fixing mismatch when localePrefix is 'as-needed' and default locale has no URL prefix
- Header: replace broken window.location.pathname.replace with next-intl router.replace
  for locale switching (old approach failed when /en wasn't in the URL)
- BottomNav overlay: fix inset-0 + left-1/2 covering only half the screen
- ShopGrid: convert category filter from useState to useSearchParams for deep-linkable
  URLs (e.g. /shop?category=Jamdani)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Gmail sync overwriting saved CRM categorization

The sync was always including client_id in the update row (even as null),
which overwrote user's manually-saved CRM categorization on every refresh.
Rules-based end_client/campaign_id/opportunity_id had the same issue.

Now for existing threads, CRM fields are only set if currently empty:
- client_id: only set from email match if not already saved
- end_client: only set from rule if not already saved
- campaign_id: only set from rule if not already saved
- opportunity_id: only set from rule if not already saved

New threads still get all CRM fields set from email matching and rules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix agent v2 file writing in GitHub Actions

The agent was failing to write digest files because permission_mode was set to
"acceptEdits" which requires interactive approval. In GitHub Actions, this caused
the agent to fail and fall back to fallback.py.

Changes:
- Set permission_mode to "acceptAll" to allow writes without prompts
- Added "Write" to allowed_tools list

This fixes the issue where digests were being sent by fallback instead of the
curated v2 agent.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
feat(indent): add quantifier body indentation for `\E` and `\A`

Lines following a quantifier (`\E`/`\A`) ending with `:` now indent the
body to `quant_col + 3`, aligning with the bound variable position past
`\E ` or `\A `. This matches Lamport's formatting style (e.g. Paxos
spec). Subsequent `/\` and `\/` lines align via operator search.

commit.message:
Fix popup overlay showing sitewide — hide via inline style in HTML

Root cause: the exit-intent script queried .da-overlay before that
element existed in the DOM (script at line ~1573, overlay at ~1719),
so querySelector returned null and hide() never hid the overlay.
The overlay CSS (position:fixed, rgba dark background, z-index:99)
then covered the entire page making it unclickable.

Fix: add display:none directly to the inline style attribute of both
the popup wrapper and overlay div in HTML, so they are never visible
regardless of script timing.

commit.message:
fix(eval): abort cleanly on proxy down — don't trip circuit breaker on service restart

When the service receives SIGTERM, the FastAPI proxy shuts down before background
eval threads finish. Previously, ConnectionRefused → returned (None, None) → failed++
→ circuit breaker tripped at 31% threshold, marking the run as failed with a
misleading circuit_breaker error.

Fix:
- Add _ProxyDownError exception class for proxy-level connectivity failures
- Catch httpx.ConnectError in _call_proxy() and raise _ProxyDownError (not generic except)
- Catch _ProxyDownError in run_eval_generate() and run_eval_judge() loops: mark run
  failed with error="proxy_unavailable", return immediately — no failed++ increment

Circuit breaker preserved for actual Ollama quality failures (bad/empty responses).
Deployment restarts now produce a clear "proxy_unavailable" error instead of the
misleading circuit_breaker error.

Tests: 3 new (TestProxyDown) — ConnectError raises _ProxyDownError, generate loop
aborts without circuit breaker, judge loop aborts cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Mesh API: prefer FQDN hostnames where available

Local node tries `hostname -f` for FQDN. Remote nodes prefer the SSH
config host name (FQDN) over the remote's short hostname. Remote also
tries `hostname -f` as fallback.

commit.message:
Add tonic floors to neuromodulators: eta/decay/expl can never reach zero

Like tonic dopamine/NE/ACh — always-on baseline that evolution can't eliminate.
eta=[0.1,1.0], decay=[0.1,1.0], expl=[0.1+noise,0.5+noise]. Hard floors
prevent the meta-learning conservatism trap where backprop pushes plasticity
toward zero because "write less = lower short-term loss."
